tasks.description: New PDF sections: executive summary, journey map, journey sentiment, perspective comparison. Add 4 new render functions to src/lib/export/pdf.ts that the export dialog can invoke. Executive summary = key metrics card page (sections, steps, maturity avg, gap count, cost totals). Journey map = canvas snapshot + touchpoint table (reuse journey-pdf.ts patterns). Journey sentiment = sentiment distribution bar + pain ranking table. Perspective comparison = divergence table with ratings per perspective.
acceptance_criteria: New exported function renderExecutiveSummary(pdf, options) in src/lib/export/pdf.ts — adds page with key metrics: total sections, total steps, avg maturity, steps below target, total monthly hours, total monthly cost, top 3 gaps
New exported function renderJourneyMap(pdf, options) — adds journey canvas snapshot page + touchpoint details table (reuse patterns from journey-pdf.ts)
New exported function renderJourneySentiment(pdf, options) — adds sentiment distribution bar + pain point ranking table (reuse journey-pdf.ts patterns)

New exported function renderPerspectiveComparison(pdf, options) — adds perspective comparison table with columns: element name, perspective A rating, perspective B rating, divergence. Highlight rows with divergence >= 2
Export dialog SECTION_GROUPS marks executiveSummary, journeyMap, journeySentiment, perspectiveComparison as available: true
canvas-view.tsx onExport handler reads ExportConfig and calls the new render functions when their flags are true
Each new section starts on a new page with dark background and consistent heading style matching existing sections

patterns_to_follow: Summary stat cards: roundedRect with bold number + small uppercase label
Page overflow: check y > pageHeight - margin - 5 before each row
Color indicators: 1.2 radius circles before text values
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — client functions return T directly

tasks.special_instructions: This is the largest sub-task of FEAT-043. Create a new file src/lib/export/enhanced-pdf-sections.ts for the 4 new render functions to keep pdf.ts manageable. Each function takes (pdf: jsPDF, data, pageConfig) and adds pages. The export dialog already has the section toggles — just change available: false to available: true for the 4 sections. canvas-view.tsx must fetch the additional data (perspectives, annotations, journey data) when those sections are enabled and pass it to the render functions. Use the EXISTING patterns from journey-pdf.ts and comparison-pdf.ts — do NOT invent new styling. Executive summary is pure computation from steps/sections already available. Journey sections need stages/touchpoints data. Perspective comparison needs perspectives + annotations data.

tasks.description: Add active indicator dot on paintbrush button when coloring rules are active. Small teal dot in top-right corner of button.
acceptance_criteria: Paintbrush button in canvas-view.tsx shows a small dot indicator (e.g., 6px teal circle) when any active coloring rule exists
Dot is positioned at top-right corner of the button (absolute positioning within relative container)
No dot when coloringRules is empty or all rules are inactive (is_active=false)

patterns_to_follow: Comment/task count badges use absolute positioned small circles

tasks.special_instructions: canvas-view.tsx already has coloringRules state. Check if any rule has is_active=true. Add a relative wrapper around the paintbrush button and an absolute-positioned small teal dot. NOTE: Slot 1 also owns canvas-view.tsx. This slot should ONLY touch the paintbrush button JSX — add a relative wrapper and dot indicator. Do NOT modify any export-related code in canvas-view.tsx.

tasks.description: Fix export dialog preset names: Quick Summary → Executive Summary, Full Report → Full Audit, Custom → Gap Report (or add as 4th). Also fix BUG-039: add DialogDescription or aria-describedby={undefined} to suppress Radix warnings.
acceptance_criteria: Preset buttons labeled: 'Executive Summary', 'Full Audit', 'Gap Report' matching FEAT-043 spec
Executive Summary preset: canvasSnapshot + gapAnalysis + executiveSummary (when available)
Full Audit preset: all available sections enabled
Gap Report preset: canvasSnapshot + gapAnalysis + perspectiveComparison + improvements (when available)
No Radix aria-describedby console warnings when dialog opens (BUG-039)

patterns_to_follow: Existing Radix Dialog pattern: DialogDescription already imported and used
learnings_relevant: BUG-023 pattern: use DialogPrimitive.Title for a11y

tasks.special_instructions: Rename the 3 presets to match spec. Update the config objects: EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_CONFIG should enable executiveSummary + canvasSnapshot + gapAnalysis. FULL_AUDIT_CONFIG should enable everything. GAP_REPORT_CONFIG should enable canvasSnapshot + gapAnalysis + perspectiveComparison + improvements. The preset type should change from 'quick'|'full'|'custom' to 'executive'|'full'|'gap'. For BUG-039, a DialogDescription already exists in the dialog — the warning may be from a second nested dialog or from the content not being properly associated. Check if aria-describedby={undefined} on DialogContent suppresses it, or ensure DialogDescription is rendered. IMP-095 (auto-Custom) is already handled by the existing handleToggle which calls setActivePreset('custom') — verify this still works after rename.

shared_files.note: canvas-view.tsx is shared between slot 1 and slot 2 — slot 1 owns export logic changes, slot 2 owns paintbrush button indicator only. Non-overlapping regions.

testing_plan.regression_reason: Last regression was iteration 120 (5 iterations ago). Risk score is 1. No regression trigger.
